calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys? Fees Incurred In Defeating Petition To Vacate Or, In The Alternative, Correct Arbitration Award And Obtaining Confirmation Of Award
Off Calendar Jan-19-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Attys. CHET A. KRONENBERG, Esq. and JONATHAN C. SANDERS, Esq. of Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP (Tel: 310-407-7500) for petitioners;
Attys. PATRICK LOI, Esq. and ALEX REESE, Esq. of Farella Braun + Martel LLP (Tel: 415-954-4400) for respondent Albert Cha.

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT ALBERT CHA's Motion For Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Defeating Petition To Vacate Or, In The Alternative, Correct Arbitration Award And Obtaining Confirmation Of Award.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Respondent's "motion for attorneys' fees incurred in defeating petition to vacate or, in the alternative, correct arbitration award and obtaining confirmation of award" is denied.

The parties agree that the relevant provision of their arbitration agreement is: "The arbitrator may award one of the parties all or part of its costs and expenses of arbitrating (including attorneys' fees and disbursements) if he or she concludes that it is appropriate." (Mot. 10:8-12; Opp. 7:26-27; Sanders Dec. Ex B 13.4.)
In contrast, the parties' arbitration agreement does not authorize a court to award any attorney fees.

Respondent's most relevant case is Ajida Technologies, Inc. v. Roos Instruments, Inc. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 534. There, attorney fees were awarded on appeal, but that was because the arbitrators' award so provided. (Id. at 551.) Here, the arbitrator's award does not purport to extend the parties' attorney fee provision to future proceedings.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR No. 13859, Email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Tel: 714-471-2597 (302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Or, In The Alternative, Correct Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Chet A. Kronenberg, Esq. (310.407.7557; ckronenberg@stblaw.com) and Jonathan Sanders, Esq. (650.251.5071; JSanders@stblaw.com) of Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP for Petitioner.
Alex Reese, Esq. (415.954.4400; areese@fbm.com) of Farella Braun + Martel LLP for Respondent, Albert Chan.
Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 (415.274.9977; sheilapham28@gmail.com) appeared via ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONERS VIVO CAPITAL LLC and VIVO VENTURES VII, LLC'S Petition To Vacate Or, In The Alternative, Correct Arbitration Award is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 (415.274.9977; sheilapham28@gmail.com). =(302/RBU)

***Order DENYING PETITION TO VACATE OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, CORRECT ARBITRATION AWARD is signed after hearing.***
